Hi Sergio, 
 
Thank you for the update. Based on the MOU, my understanding of the next steps is that Centrilogic will initiate warm introductions for eligible opportunities to Aptum, and my team should not engage directly with clients unless that introduction has been made by Centrilogic. Please let me know if that understanding is correct. 
 
I also understand there has been one exception, as Azamour was already introduced to Aptum and is currently engaged with our local team. 
 
My team will remain on standby next week and is ready for you to initiate the introductions. Please coordinate with me and Frederic Gingras as needed. 
 
@Adam Nelson: Could you please assist Sergio with accessing the file in our tenant? If I recall correctly, you originally created the shared folder. 
 
Thanks, 
MA
 

Marc Alex Forget

**
To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Aptum operates data centre facilities in Heathrow and Portsmouth and is available to consult directly with affected customers to assess requirements and discuss transition options.
Available solutions include colocation services, private cloud offerings, and related infrastructure options. Customers are under no obligation to use Aptum and remain free to pursue alternative arrangements with a provider of their choosing. This option is offered to provide flexibility and additional support during the transition process.

Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.
SERGIO BERNARDOU
Project Management Consultant

items.subject: Microsoft PSfP Premier Partner Forum - April 2026 - M365 E7 - Follow-Up
items.body_text:
[Michael Wangenstein]
Good Afternoon Team,

Thank you for taking the time to attend April's Premier Partner Forum around M365 E7!

The deck presented today is attached, and the recording can be found here. As a reminder, you need to have been registered for the event in order to access the recording.

Recap of the meeting:

Microsoft 365 E7

Strategic Positioning of E7: E7 is not just an incremental SKU but represents a shift to AI-embedded workflows, supporting the transition from tools that assist to systems that actively participate in work. The suite is designed to address the acceleration in AI agent adoption, with industry projections highlighting rapid growth in enterprise AI integration.

Intelligence + Trust Framework: Indranil stressed that E7’s core value lies in combining advanced AI capabilities with enterprise-grade security, compliance, and governance. This dual focus is essential to mitigate new risks introduced by AI while maximizing business value.

Three Pillars of E7:

AI in the Flow of Work: AI is deeply integrated into familiar productivity tools (Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint), ensuring users benefit from AI without disrupting established workflows.

Empowering Innovation: E7 supports a flexible ecosystem of Microsoft, partner, and custom-built agents, enabling organizations to address unique business needs and accelerate adoption through both low-code and pro-code approaches.

Observability and Governance: The suite introduces robust observability, allowing organizations to inventory, monitor, and govern all agents and their data interactions, ensuring transparency and accountability.

Agent 365: Security and Governance

Agent 365 Overview: A core component of E7, Agent 365 provides a unified control plane for managing AI agents, treating them as first-class security principals. It delivers agent registry, access control, visualization, interoperability, and security/threat protection.

Identity and Lifecycle Management: Agents receive unique identities (Agent ID) and are subject to lifecycle management, sponsorship, and access governance, mirroring controls applied to human users.

Data Security and Compliance: Microsoft Purview extends data protection, DLP, and compliance controls to agents, ensuring sensitive data remains governed regardless of whether accessed by users or AI.

Threat Protection: Microsoft Defender now includes agent-specific posture management and threat detection, integrating agents into the organization’s overall security framework.

Licensing and Adoption

SKU Structure: E7 bundles Microsoft 365 E5, Copilot, Agent 365, and Entra Suite, offering a cost-effective package compared to purchasing components separately. Pricing is per user per month, with Teams included or excluded as needed.

Licensing Questions: Indranil clarified that licensing is believed to be user-based, not agent-based, but recommended confirmation with account teams for specific scenarios.

Migration Guidance: Transitioning clients from E5 or Copilot to E7 requires coordination with account teams, as renewal dates and existing agreements may affect migration paths.

Additional E7 Resources:

Introducing Microsoft 365 E7: The Frontier Suite

M365 E7 Launch Kit

M365 E7 CSP Pricelist (Available April 1st)

M365 E7 Prom Readiness Guide (Available April 1st)

Microsoft 365 Blog | Latest Product Updates and Insights

If you have any further questions, please feel free to reach out and let me know!

Thank you,

Michael Wangenstein 
Sr. Customer Success Account Manager 
Microsoft Services – North America SMC – Partners 
Mobile: 914.597.7686 
Michael.Wangenstein@microsoft.com

Si les bases du marketing par courriel demeurent, leur importance s’intensifie : les contenus pertinents sont favorisés, tandis que les messages génériques perdent en visibilité. Cette conférence vous aidera à adapter vos stratégies pour mieux performer dans cet environnement et maximiser l’impact de vos communications.
Cette conférence vous permettra de : 
Comprendre comment l’IA priorise les courriels dans la boîte de réception
Apprendre à concevoir des courriels que l’IA comprend et recommande
Identifier les pratiques qui renforcent l’engagement des audiences
Tirer profit d’une stratégie omnicanale pour amplifier l’impact des communications

 

L’IA est déjà présente dans les organisations, mais ses bénéfices restent difficiles à mesurer et à déployer à grande échelle. Plus qu’un enjeu technologique, elle révèle la qualité des données, des systèmes et de la gouvernance. Une approche pragmatique permet de mieux investir et d’en faire un réel levier de valeur.
 Cette conférence vous permettra de : 
Comprendre pourquoi des initiatives d’IA produisent des résultats mesurables et d’autres non
Identifier des obstacles organisationnels qui limitent l’impact réel de l’IA
Structurer des données, systèmes et processus pour opérer l’IA à l’échelle
Investir avec discernement pour transformer l’IA en performance opérationnelle durable

 

Revivez notre précédente édition en images
 
 
Plongez au cœur de notre dernière édition et (re)découvrez les moments forts qui l’ont marquée. Conférences inspirantes, échanges enrichissants et ambiance unique : revivez l'édition 2025 en images.

items.subject: RE: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance
items.body_text:
[Melvin Ching]
Hi all,
 
We’ve cleared up the confusion. There are 2 areas where we need to meet skilling requirements. One is for the actual Specialization pre-requisite and the second is to obtain Solutions Partner Designation.
 
The Azure Database Administrator Associate (DP-300) is for the Specialization pre-requisite and we need this. All the AZ-700 certs go towards Solutions Partner Designation and we are in good shape there with the addition of some more Azure Virtual Desktop candidates.
 
Please review with your teams to see if there is anyone who will be interested in DP-300.
 
@Jason Auer @Andrei Ianouchkevitch, Charles and Marc expressed interest in the past.
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,

Melvin Ching

Director, Business Assurance
+1 (416) 840-7102
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Melvin Ching 
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 5:43 PM
To: Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>; Ben Kennedy <Ben.Kennedy@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Simon Godard <Simon.Godard@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance
 
I will check in with Andy.
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,

Melvin Ching

Director, Business Assurance
+1 (416) 840-7102
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 5:25 PM
To: Melvin Ching <Melvin.Ching@aptum.com>; Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>; Ben Kennedy <Ben.Kennedy@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Kemp@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Simon Godard <Simon.Godard@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance
 

Hey Melvin, we have a few others Jacob is studying for AZ700 and Traci is studying for Virtual desktop.   Can you confirm the requirement on the DP-300 based on my conversation with Andy, my understanding was that one was no longer needed. 

 

Jason Auer

Manager, Customer Support
+1 (678) 365-2800
aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

From: Melvin Ching <Melvin.Ching@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 5:19 PM
To: Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>; Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>; Ben Kennedy <Ben.Kennedy@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Kemp@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Simon Godard <Simon.Godard@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance 

 
Good afternoon leaders,
 
Thank you for your continued support in our efforts to get Microsoft Specialization status.
 
I wanted to provide an update on the skill certification ask from before. You can also view the Confluence page linked below for more details.
 
Currently, we have the list of individuals below that are planning to obtain certification:

Name
Certification
Target date

Andy Petterson
Azure Network Engineer Associate (AZ-700)
30th May 2026

Robert Grafton
Azure Network Engineer Associate (AZ-700)
30th May 2026

David Taylor
Azure Network Engineer Associate (AZ-700)
30th May 2026

Chris Templeman
Azure Network Engineer Associate (AZ-700)
30th May 2026

Justin Ownby
Azure Virtual Desktop Specialty (AZ-140)
30th May 2026
 
We do still have a requirement for one person to obtain Azure Database Administrator Associate (DP-300), please review and discuss with your teams on who would be best suited or interested. Please let me know right away if you have a candidate.
 
If you a team member not on the list above, please let me know. Please continue to encourage your team members as we need redundancy in case of changes.
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,

Melvin Ching
Director, Business Assurance
+1 (416) 840-7102
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Melvin Ching
Sent: Wednesday, February 4, 2026 3:38 PM
To: Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>; Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>; Ben Kennedy <Ben.Kennedy@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Lacie-Ellen Kemp <Lacie.Kemp@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Simon Godard <Simon.Godard@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance
 
Good afternoon leaders,
 
Thank you for your attention to the request I raised last December.
 
I would like to inform you that there has been some changes in our strategy for certification and the list of skill certifications we now need. Please visit the Confluence page
for the updated list.
 
If you already have (or team members) that have already started with a previously identified certification, please do not discourage them from completing it. We could still use it in the future for another program/designation.
 
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to me. Please also let me know if you have a team member that will be pursuing a certification from the list (not already noted in the page).
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,

Melvin Ching
Director, Business Assurance
+1 (416) 840-7102
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Melvin Ching
Sent: Wednesday, December 3, 2025 5:14 PM
To: Andrei Ianouchkevitch <Andrei.Ianouchkevitch@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>; Jason Auer <Jason.Auer@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>; Ben Kennedy <Ben.Kennedy@aptum.com>; Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Lacie-Ellen Kemp <Lacie.Kemp@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Simon Godard <Simon.Godard@aptum.com>
Cc: Nikki Thind <Nikki.Thind@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: Microsoft skill certification - need your assistance
 
Good afternoon leaders,
 
As you know, we have a strategic initiative to replace our Azure Expert MSP certification with a different Microsoft Specialization certification. As part of the new program, we need to have a different set of skill certifications within the company.
 
For the most updated list and priority, please visit this new Confluence page that I will be maintaining - https://aptum.atlassian.net/wiki/x/GQBEGwE
 
I would like to encourage you to please have a conversation with your respective teams and promote our need as a great personal development opportunity. We have an aggressive timeline and need the certifications within the next 1-3 months if possible. If you have identified candidates, please let me know and I will add them to my tracking.
 
For questions regarding training resources and potential reimbursement of exam costs, please contact Nikki. Any other questions please feel free to contact me.
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,
 

Melvin Ching
Director, Business Assurance
+1 (416) 840-7102
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Commercial

Submit Your Nominations Today!

Do you know a young person in the real estate business who stands out because of his or her talent, drive and fresh ideas? Do you have a rising star in your midst? 
 
Please consider nominating them for one of Connect Commercial Real Estate’s 10th annual Next Generation Awards. We’ll consider candidates in all sectors of commercial estate—from leasing and development to investment and finance.
 
Based on your nominations, we’ll choose the most talented young pros working in real estate. The winners will be announced in June.

items.subject: Expedient EMERGENCY Service Maintenance Notification (Maintenance Window Extended) - Elastic Cloud Enterprise Infrastructure Maintenance Change #627913
items.body_text:
[no-reply@expedient.com]
Adam Nelson,

In an effort to maintain the highest quality of service, periodically it is necessary to conduct maintenance activities that affect our service infrastructure. 

Please find the details of a scheduled maintenance event below. 
Due to the length of time for this upgrade, Expedient Engineers have extended the previously communicated maintenance window again. Below please find the updated maintenance window.

Change Control #: 627913
Description: EMERGENCY Elastic Enterprise Cloud Infrastructure Maintenance
Date / Time: 2026-04-16 17:00:00 - 2026-04-27 12:00:00%

__________________________________________
Sent: Thursday, April 16 2026 10:50 AM EDT
Subject: Expedient EMERGENCY Service Maintenance Notification - Elastic Cloud Enterprise Infrastructure Maintenance Change #627913

Change Control #: 627913
Description: EMERGENCY Elastic Enterprise Cloud Infrastructure Maintenance
Date / Time: Thursday, April 16 2026 5:00 PM EDT - April 20 2026 12:00 PM EDT

Because of timeout issues encountered during the completion of Change Control #623679, Expedient has opted to schedule an extended window to ensure that snapshot operations and upgrades have ample time to complete.

Maintenance Details: This emergency maintenance event has been scheduled to re-attempt an upgrade of the infrastructure supporting the Elastic Cloud Enterprise to the latest version. This is a continuation of work that was attempted in Change Control #623679. The Elastic Cloud Enterprise is used to deliver your Elastic as a Service, Expedient CTRL, and/or Elastic Security services. 

Client Impact: There is no anticipated client impact for this maintenance event, as the Elastic Cloud Enterprise environment includes multiple redundant devices. Change activities inherently increase the risk of unanticipated impact, which is why we are providing you with this informational notification. The actual impact should not exceed the scheduled time frame. 
We are committed to ensuring that both your service and our expertise are always available. For inquiries regarding this work, please submit a request to the Support Management Console (SMC) referencing the change number above. If you do not yet have access to the SMC, please contact our support center at 1-888-227-9400.

Thank you for your continued business.

Regards,

The Expedient Operations Support Center

Please note that all times listed above are Eastern Time. To convert to the local time of your affected service location, please reference the following link: https://support.expedient.com/help/timezone 
You are receiving this email because you are a client contact for Aptum. If you don't want to receive this type of email in the future, please configure your email and recorded telephone message notification settings at https://support.expedient.com/settings. 
© 2026 Expedient

You don't often get email from awards@globaltechinsider.com. Learn why this is important 

Hi Adam, An award nomination has been received courtesy of Global Tech Insider Magazine. 

Hi Adam,
 
I’ve been trying to get in touch to make sure you are aware of the nomination received by CloudOps in the 2026 Global Tech Insider Awards, courtesy of Global Tech Insider Magazine.
 
To ensure your place on the shortlist, please confirm by clicking the 'Join Shortlist' button below:

Join Shortlist 

If you’d rather not be included this year:

Defer Nomination 

Throughout the whole process there are no obligatory expenses or time commitments. Our awards programme recognises deserving companies and is not built on a pay-to-play basis. Including yourself is a really great way to boost the profile of your company and all awardees are offered a complimentary package to help do exactly this.
 
If you are successful, we do offer various packages including editorial content to provide additional support and increase your visibility - but these are completely optional!

If you need any further information regarding the Global Tech Insider Awards, kindly visit our website or reach out to me or one of my colleagues in the Nominations Team. We’re here to assist you at every step.

Riyanka Thomas

Subject: Re: today's incident 
 
Just a quick follow up as I understand that Andy - you are meeting David and Rob later today.

The short explanation is that my finance, specifically supply chain team was not coordinating with our operations team, as they were under the assumption that there are no operational implications of changing the distributor. They were anxious to make sure by start of next billing month the discounts and supplier were in place. Unfortunately they didn't coordinate with the operations team who would have treated this via change management process and involved you for awareness and approvals (and tracked it in an appropriate change management ticket).

I have a new VP on the finance side and we have all put heads together around this, and put in place process to make sure this can't happen again.

Andy - Rob and David will be able to take you through it. Again apologies for the disruption. We have everything well in hard now but I'll let you be the judge of that. I hope you have a great evening together.

ian

President & CEO

I will standby for your assessment.

Cheers.

-AA

From: Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 14:05
To: Andy Alvarado <andy.alvarado@basis.com>
Cc: Michael Freiman <michael.freiman@basis.com>; David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>
Subject: today's incident 
 
Hi Andy,

The matter of an unauthorized change management with your AWS account today has been raised to me.

I understand there was some motivation to get the transition to the new distributor (TD Synnex) in place to address the concerns with Ingram's past behaviour, ensuring there is no root access from distribution, and also to make sure we can pass on the increased margin once we hit May invoicing.

In doing so it appears the transition to the new distributor was initiated without your awareness. While this should have no tangible impact on your environments operationally, this is a completely unacceptable situation and should have been performed via scheduled change management with your approval and involvement.

Adam Nelson our VP Operations promptly escalated this issue to me and I have asked him to review and advise me personally as to root cause and remediation.

I want to apologize sincerely for the situation, and will circle back once we understand what happened and why, and what we propose doing about it.

 cc David and Michael

Ian

items.subject: Important notice about your AWS Account regarding VPN connections [AWS Account: 120569625889, VPN Connection: vpn-087ce2d7eec1fe3d8, Tunnel Outside IP: 98.84.60.210]
items.body_text:
[Amazon Web Services]
You don't often get email from health@aws.com. Learn why this is important 

AWS Health Event

 

View in Notification Center

Important notice about your AWS Account regarding VPN connections [AWS Account: 120569625889, VPN Connection: vpn-087ce2d7eec1fe3d8, Tunnel Outside IP: 98.84.60.210]

View details in service console 

Hello, 

You are receiving this message because your VPN Connection vpn-087ce2d7eec1fe3d8 in the us-east-1 Region had a momentary lapse of redundancy as one of two tunnel endpoints (Tunnel Outside IP: 98.84.60.210) was replaced. Connectivity on the second tunnel was not affected during this time. Both tunnels are now operating normally. 

Replacements can occur for several reasons, and be initiated either by AWS or when you modify your VPN Connection [1]. AWS-initiated replacement reasons include health, software upgrades, and when underlying hardware is retired. 

This replacement was AWS Initiated. 

AWS Site-to-Site VPN includes two tunnels so that you can maintain a highly available VPN connection. If you have configured your VPN Customer Gateway to use both tunnels, then your VPN Connection will have utilized the alternate tunnel during the replacement process. For more on tunnel endpoint replacements, please see our documentation (2). If you have not configured your VPN Customer Gateway to use both tunnels, then your VPN Connection may have been interrupted during the replacement. We encourage you to configure your router to use both tunnels. You can obtain the VPN Connection configuration recommendations for several types of VPN devices from the AWS Management Console [3]. On the "Amazon VPC" tab, select "VPN Connections". Then highlight the VPN Connection and choose "Download Configuration". 

[1] https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpn/latest/s2svpn/endpoint-replacements.html 
[2] https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpn/latest/s2svpn/monitoring-vpn-health-events.html 
[3] https://console.aws.amazon.com 

.
